> Latest claude-goal POSIX bang reserved word detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop while root launch work remained blocked by the current dirty handoff boundary. Official GNU Bash docs define pipelines in the form `[time [-p]] [!] command1 ...` and `&&` / `||` lists as left-associative conditional execution. Fresh marker proof showed `bash -lc '! python3 .../claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, `bash -lc 'echo ready; ! python3 ...'`, and `bash -lc '! time python3 ...'` write the stop-hook marker while both detector copies returned false; companion false positives `! true && python3 ...` and `! false || python3 ...` do not write a marker but were treated as reachable before the fix. > Latest claude-goal POSIX static conditional detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop while root launch work remained blocked by the current dirty handoff boundary. Official GNU Bash docs define `&&` / `||` as left-associative AND/OR lists where the right command runs only when the left command exits with the required success/failure status. Fresh marker proof showed `bash -lc 'true || python3 .../claude_goal.py stop-hook'` and `bash -lc 'false && python3 .../claude_goal.py stop-hook'` do not write a marker while both detector copies returned true; controls `false || python3 ...`, `true && python3 ...`, and `echo ready; python3 ...` wrote the marker and stay true. Added regression coverage in `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py` for legacy and structured Bash static false conditionals. > Latest claude-goal POSIX command/exec shell builtin detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop. Official POSIX/GNU docs confirm `command [-p] command_name [argument...]` executes the named command while `-v/-V` only report it, and `exec [command [argument...]]` executes by replacing the shell. Fresh marker probes reproduced that `command python3 .tmp/probe_command_wrapper/claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `command -p python3 ...`, and `exec python3 ...` return `0` and write marker text `stop-hook`; `command -v`, `command echo`, and `exec echo` write no marker. Before the fix, both detector copies returned false for the command/exec Python positives. Fixed both detector copies in `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by reusing the existing POSIX shell builtin wrapper stripper for top-level legacy hook command strings before checking for the goal Stop hook, without treating direct structured `command` hooks as executable. Added regression coverage in `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py`. Verification passed marker probe, focused red/green pytest (`2 passed`, `9 passed`), related wrapper pytest (`14 passed`, `45 passed`), Ruff check/format, `py_compile`, path-limited diff-check, full nested pytest (`462 passed in 8m49s`), graph risk `0.00`, and evidence note `.tmp/claude-goal-command-exec-builtin-wrapper-current.md`. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, graph process kill, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal env -S reset Windows PATH detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop. Fresh WSL marker probes showed split-string reset positives `/usr/bin/env -S '-i python3' /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env -S '- python3' ...`, `/usr/bin/env -S '--ignore-environment python3' ...`, and `/usr/bin/env -vS '-i python3' ...` execute the stop-hook marker and detect true. Reproduced the actual bug with `/usr/bin/env -S '-i PATH=C:/Python313 python3' /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook`: command failed with rc `127` and no marker, but both detector copies returned true. Root cause: `_legacy_python_launcher_prefix()` checked Windows drive PATH operands for normal env wrappers but not for operands embedded inside `env -S` split strings. Fixed both detector copies in `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by computing split-string env operands and rejecting them when `_env_rest_has_windows_drive_path(...)` is true. Added shell-form and structured regression coverage in `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py`. Verification passed marker probe, focused env reset pytest (`2 passed`, `7 passed`), related detector pytest (`74 passed, 324 deselected`), Ruff check/format, `py_compile`, path-limited diff-check, full nested pytest (`451 passed in 8m58s`), graph risk `0.00`, and cleanup of this loop's basetemps. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, graph process kill, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal direct env reset Python detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop. Reproduced with WSL marker probes that `/usr/bin/env -i python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env - python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and `/usr/bin/env --ignore-environment python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook` execute the stop-hook marker while both detector copies were false. Control probes stayed aligned: PATH reset with `/usr/bin:/bin` was already true, Windows drive PATH reset failed and stayed false, and `env -i echo ... stop-hook` stayed false. Root cause: reset-without-PATH guards were too broad for GNU env command lookup, plus env assignment values such as `PATH=/c/Python313` could be mistaken for Python launcher tokens. Fixed both detector copies in `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by relaxing only reset-without-PATH while preserving Windows drive PATH rejection and by skipping env assignment tokens during launcher scan. Added/updated shell-form, structured, and migration tests in `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py`. Verification passed marker probe, focused pytest (`2 passed`, `5 passed`, migration `1 passed`), related detector pytest (`57 passed, 339 deselected`), Ruff check/format, `py_compile`, path-limited diff-check, full nested pytest retry (`449 passed in 9m48s`; first full attempt had one transient Windows `CreateProcess` PermissionError that passed on rerun), graph risk `0.00`, and cleanup of this loop's basetemps. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, graph process kill, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal bash option-value shell wrapper detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop. Reproduced with WSL marker probes that `bash -o pipefail -c 'python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'` and `bash -O extglob -c 'python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'` execute the stop-hook marker while both detectors were false; also reproduced false positives where invalid `bash -o -c 'python3 ... stop-hook'` and `bash -oc 'python3 ... stop-hook'` returned rc `2`, wrote no marker, but detectors were true. Fixed both detector copies in `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` so `-o/+o/-O/+O` require and skip a non-option value, and short clusters only count as `-c` when `c` appears before any `o` value-taking option. Added shell-form and structured regression coverage in `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py`. Verification passed post-fix marker probe, focused pytest (`2 passed`, `5 passed`), related detector pytest (`32 passed, 333 deselected`), Ruff check/format, `py_compile`, path-limited diff-check, full nested pytest (`418 passed` in 7m57s), graph risk `0.00`, and cleanup. > Latest claude-goal POSIX find-exec detector loop: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued only the nested `claude-goal/` autonomous debugging loop. Reproduced with a WSL marker script that `find /tmp -maxdepth 0 -exec python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook \;` executes the stop-hook marker, while `find ... -exec echo ... \;` and `find --help -exec ... \;` do not. Root cause: stop-hook detection must treat `find -exec` / `-execdir` command clauses as execution wrappers only when a valid terminator is present, and shell segmentation must keep the `;` terminator inside the find segment. Current detector copies in `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` parse these clauses; regression coverage in `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py` covers shell-form and structured positives plus echo/help/missing-terminator negatives. > Latest claude-goal nested POSIX shell -c wrapper fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested stop-hook detector loop after POSIX grouping and structured execution-prefix fixes. Reproduced that `bash -lc "sh -c 'python3 /mnt/c/tmp/.../claude_goal.py stop-hook'"` and `bash -lc "bash -c 'python3 /mnt/c/tmp/.../claude_goal.py stop-hook'"` execute a marker hook while doctor/installer detection returned false. Confirmed nested `sh -c 'echo ... stop-hook'` and `bash -c 'echo ... stop-hook'` forms remain ignored. Root cause: POSIX shell tail splitting stripped unmatched inner trailing quotes after removing outer quotes, and shell segments were not recursively inspected when they themselves invoked `sh/bash -c`. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now strip only matching outer quote pairs and recursively evaluate nested POSIX shell `-c` segments after existing wrapper stripping. Tests add nested shell-form and structured bash positives plus nested echo negatives. Verification passed nested shell marker probe, detector focused pytest (`5 passed`), structured execution-prefix focused pytest (`10 passed`), related detector pytest (`16 passed, 334 deselected`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, full nested pytest (`403 passed` in 10m57s), clean path-limited diff-check, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp/probe cleanup. `find -exec` remains an observed inventory candidate only. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, graph process kill, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal hook detector POSIX ionice wrapper fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested stop-hook detector loop after the POSIX `flock` wrapper fix. Reproduced that `bash -lc 'ionice python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, `bash -lc 'ionice -c2 -n7 python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, direct `/usr/bin/ionice -c2 -n7 python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and `/usr/bin/env /usr/bin/ionice -c2 -n7 python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook` execute a marker hook while doctor/installer detection returned false. Tests add direct/bash/env `ionice` positives plus `ionice echo` negatives. Verification passed direct detector probe, focused pytest (`5 passed`), related detector pytest (`6 passed, 332 deselected`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, full nested pytest (`391 passed` in 10m24s), clean path-limited diff-check, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp/probe cleanup. > Latest claude-goal hook detector POSIX setsid wrapper fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested stop-hook detector loop after the POSIX `stdbuf` wrapper fix. Reproduced that `bash -lc 'setsid -w python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, `bash -lc 'setsid -f -w python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, and `bash -lc 'echo ready; setsid -w python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'` execute a marker hook while doctor/installer detection returned false; confirmed `setsid -w echo ... stop-hook` and unknown-option `setsid --bad-option python3 ... stop-hook` do not run the hook and remain ignored. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now strip POSIX `setsid` wrappers only for supported `-c/--ctty`, `-f/--fork`, `-w/--wait`, and `--` options before applying existing Python launcher checks. Tests add shell-form/structured positives plus `setsid echo`/unknown-option negatives. > Latest claude-goal hook detector POSIX exec option fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested stop-hook detector loop after the POSIX `command` builtin fix. Reproduced that `bash -lc 'exec -a goal-python python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, `bash -lc 'echo ready; exec -a goal-python python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, `bash -lc 'exec -c python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, `bash -lc 'exec -l python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'`, and `bash -lc 'exec -- python3 /tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook'` execute a marker hook while doctor/installer detection returned false; confirmed `exec -a ... echo` and malformed `exec -a` do not run the hook and remain ignored. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now strip POSIX `exec` options that still execute a command (`-a NAME`, `-c`, `-l`, `--`) before applying the `command` builtin/Python launcher checks. Tests add shell-form/structured `exec` option positives plus `exec -a ... echo` negatives. Verification passed direct exec-option probe, focused pytest (`5 passed`), related detector pytest (`6 passed, 332 deselected`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, full nested pytest (`391 passed`), diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, graph process kill, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal hook detector absolute cmd.exe wrapper fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` stop-hook detector loop after the absolute `env.exe` wrapper fix. Reproduced a Windows-specific false negative: structured hook command `C:\WINDOWS\system32\cmd.exe` with args `/d /s /c python claude_goal.py stop-hook` executes a temporary marker hook, while doctor/installer detection returned false. Root cause was that cmd wrapper recognition only accepted literal `cmd` / `cmd.exe`, not absolute `cmd.exe` path basenames. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now use `_is_cmd_utility_token(...)` to recognize `cmd`, `cmd.exe`, and absolute Windows cmd.exe paths while preserving `/c` position and option whitelist checks. Tests add shell-form and structured absolute `cmd.exe` positive cases. Verification passed direct absolute `cmd.exe` probe, focused pytest (`2 passed`), related cmd/env wrapper selection (`8 passed, 329 deselected`), full nested suite (`390 passed`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal hook detector absolute env.exe wrapper fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` stop-hook detector loop after the `env -` reset alias fix. First probed Python launcher options and found no actual-vs-detector mismatch, then reproduced a Windows-specific false negative: structured hook command `C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\env.exe` with args `python claude_goal.py stop-hook` and `-S "python -u" claude_goal.py stop-hook` executes the hook, while doctor/installer detection returned false. Root cause was that env wrapper recognition only accepted literal `env` and `/usr/bin/env`, not the basename of absolute `env.exe` paths. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now use `_is_env_utility_token(...)` to recognize `env`, `env.exe`, `/usr/bin/env`, and absolute Windows env.exe paths consistently, including the `-S` branch. Tests add shell-form and structured absolute `env.exe` positive cases. Verification passed direct absolute `env.exe` probe, focused pytest (`2 passed`), related env-wrapper selection (`8 passed, 329 deselected`), full nested suite (`390 passed`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal hook detector env dash reset alias fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` stop-hook detector loop after the Windows PATH false-positive fix. Used a Git-for-Windows `env.exe` actual-vs-detector matrix with a temporary marker `claude_goal.py` and reproduced that `env - PATH=/c/Users/.../Scripts python claude_goal.py stop-hook` executes the hook, while doctor/installer detection returned false. Confirmed `env - python ...` without PATH still exits `127` and must remain ignored. Root cause was that lone `-` is an `env` ignore-environment/reset alias, but both detector copies only stripped `-i` / `--ignore-environment`. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now consume `-` as a reset operand while preserving PATH and Windows-drive-path safeguards. Tests add positive `env - PATH=/c/Python313 python ...` shell/structured coverage and negative no-PATH `env - python ...` shell/structured coverage. Verification passed direct `env.exe` probe, focused pytest (`4 passed`), related env-wrapper selection (`8 passed, 329 deselected`), full nested suite (`390 passed`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp cleanup. > Latest claude-goal hook detector env reset Windows PATH false-positive fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` stop-hook detector loop after the env reset PATH false-negative fix. Reproduced with Git-for-Windows `env.exe` that `env -i PATH=C:/Users/.../Scripts python claude_goal.py stop-hook` exits `127` (`python` not found), while `env -i PATH=/c/Users/.../Scripts python ...` exits `0`; before the fix doctor/installer detection accepted both. Root cause was accepting any PATH assignment after env reset, plus joined-prefix launcher detection mistaking `PATH=/c/Python313` for a Python launcher because the path basename matched `python\d+`. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now parse PATH assignment values, reject Windows drive-path assignments inside POSIX env wrappers, preserve POSIX `/c/...` reinjection, and skip joined-prefix launcher detection when env assignments are present. Tests add negative `PATH=C:/Python313` shell/structured cases and positive `/c/Python313` reset PATH cases. Verification passed direct `env.exe` probe, focused pytest (`4 passed`), related env-wrapper selection (`8 passed, 329 deselected`), full nested suite (`390 passed`), Ruff check/format, py_compile, diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and basetemp cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal install-health schema enum unhashable fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in `claude-goal/` after the install-health `check_names` unhashable loop. Reproduced an installed skill schema tampered with `$defs.checkName.enum[0] = []`; before the fix `doctor --install --json` exited `1` with `TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'` and traceback. Root cause: `_install_health_schema_contract_mismatches(...)` compared raw schema arrays using `set(...)`, so malformed enum/required arrays with unhashable items crashed before becoming a `skill_schema_contract` health failure. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` now adds `_list_values_match_set(...)` and uses it for install-health schema required/enum contract checks, preserving duplicate enforcement for action/check enum arrays. `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` adds regression coverage for the unhashable installed schema enum. Verification passed direct repro (`rc=0`, JSON health output with `skill_schema_contract` fail detail `canonical body; $defs.checkName.enum`, no traceback), focused test (`1 passed`), related schema selection (`5 passed, 211 deselected`), full nested suite (`390 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check after formatting `claude_goal.py`, py_compile, diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and pytest basetemp cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal install-health verifier unhashable allowed-value fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in `claude-goal/` while preserving root dirty-handoff/current-head/T-251 boundaries. Confirmed the install/no-home and `~` hook path hypotheses were not bugs, then reproduced a real verifier crash by generating a healthy strict install-health artifact and tampering allowed-value fields to JSON arrays: `checks[0].name`, `checks[0].status`, `action_catalog[0].id`, and `next_action_items[0].id` all exited `1` with `TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'` plus traceback. Root cause: `_verify_install_health_allowed_value(...)` used direct `value in allowed_values` membership against sets without handling unhashable JSON values. `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` now treats membership `TypeError` as a mismatch and raises the existing domain `ValueError`; `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py` adds regression coverage for all four fields. Verification passed direct repro (`rc=2`, explicit install-health error, no traceback), focused test (`1 passed`), related verifier selection (`21 passed, 192 deselected`), full nested suite (`387 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check, py_compile, diff-check with only existing LF/CRLF warnings, graph risk `0.00`, and pytest basetemp cleanup. > Latest claude-goal env -i home-less Stop-hook import fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the nested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in `claude-goal/` after rerunning the root 0-step debug inventory/selector/readiness gates. Root workspace remains blocked on the current dirty-handoff boundary, user-owned Hanwoo T-251, unpushed current-head Actions, Blind-to-X dirty handoff, and incomplete launch audit, so no root product edit was started. Reproduced a real nested Stop-hook crash with Git for Windows `env.exe -i CLAUDE_GOAL_DB=... CLAUDE_GOAL_SESSION_ID=... <absolute python> goal/scripts/claude_goal.py stop-hook`: before the fix it exited `1` at import with `RuntimeError: Could not determine home directory` from `Path.home()`. Root cause: eager `os.environ.get(..., Path.home() / ...)` default evaluation in top-level path constants. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` now lazily resolves env paths, derives `STATE_DIR` from explicit `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB` when needed, and uses state-dir-relative fallbacks when `Path.home()` is unavailable. `tests/test_claude_goal.py` adds isolated home-less Stop-hook coverage. Verification passed direct `env.exe -i` repro (`decision=block`, objective present, stderr empty), focused pytest (`1 passed`; related `3 passed`), full nested suite (`386 passed`), Ruff check, source-scoped Ruff format check, py_compile, diff-check with LF/CRLF warnings only, and nested graph detect risk `0.00`. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal hook detector env combined short option fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. Reproduced that GNU `env` combined short-option forms `env -vu CLAUDE_GOAL_DB python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `env -vC DIR python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env -vS "python -u" claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and structured `["-vu", "CLAUDE_GOAL_DB", "python", ...]` were not counted as `/goal` hooks. Verified actual execution with Git for Windows `env.exe`: `-vu` unset the env var and executed Python, `-vC` changed directory and executed Python, and `-vS` split `python -u` and executed Python; all wrote the marker through the target script with `stop-hook`. Root cause: `env` wrapper detection only consumed separate `-v`, `-u`, `-C`, and `-S` tokens, not GNU combined short-option tokens. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now consume reproduced `-v+[uC]` wrapper operands and route `-v+S` through the existing split-string launcher path, without treating `env -vu ... echo claude_goal.py stop-hook` as a goal hook. `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover shell-form, structured, migration, and false-positive echo variants. Verification passed `env.exe` combined-option proof, manual before/after detector probes (`False False` to `True True`; combined-option echo stayed `False False`), focused doctor/installer tests (`3 passed`, `1 passed`), full nested suite after formatting (`385 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check`, nested graph detect risk `0.00`, and `.tmp/pytest-t2085-*` cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. > Latest claude-goal hook detector env chdir option fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. Reproduced that `env -C DIR python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env --chdir=DIR python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env --chdir DIR python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and structured `["-C", "DIR", "python", ...]` were not counted as `/goal` hooks. Verified actual execution with Git for Windows `env.exe`: `-C`, `--chdir=...`, and `--chdir ...` changed cwd to the target directory and wrote the marker through the Python script; `env -S` with a Windows path was excluded after reproducing an actual `invalid sequence` error. Root cause was that `env` wrapper detection did not consume `-C DIR` or `--chdir` operands before resolving the Python launcher. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now strip supported chdir operands before launcher validation, without treating `env -C ... echo claude_goal.py stop-hook` as a goal hook. `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover shell-form, structured, and false-positive echo variants. Verification passed `env.exe` proof, manual before/after detector probes (`False False` to `True True`; env/chdir echo stayed `False False`), focused doctor/installer tests (`3 passed`, `1 passed`), full nested suite (`385 passed`), Ruff format check, Ruff check, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check`, nested graph detect risk `0.00`, and `.tmp/pytest-t2082-*` cleanup. No stage, commit, push, revert, root product/browser edit, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal hook detector POSIX env-assignment shell-form fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. Reproduced that shell-form hooks with leading POSIX environment assignments, such as `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB=/tmp/goal.sqlite python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB=/tmp/goal.sqlite /usr/bin/env python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB=/tmp/goal.sqlite claude_goal.py stop-hook`, were not counted as `/goal` hooks. Verified actual execution with Git for Windows Bash: each assignment-prefixed command wrote a marker through the target script while carrying the environment value. Root cause was that the detector treated leading `NAME=value` tokens as executable prefix tokens; shell syntax treats them as environment assignments before resolving the launcher or direct script. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now strip leading POSIX env-assignment tokens only for shell-form command detection before reusing existing direct-script and launcher validation. `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover Python, `/usr/bin/env python`, and direct-script assignment-prefixed variants. > Latest claude-goal hook detector direct shebang shell-form fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. Reproduced that shell-form hooks such as `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `"goal/scripts/claude_goal.py" stop-hook`, and `C:/tmp/claude_goal.py stop-hook` were not counted as `/goal` hooks. Verified actual shebang execution with Git for Windows Bash: a temporary executable `claude_goal.py` with `#!/usr/bin/env python` wrote the marker when invoked as `claude_goal.py stop-hook`. Root cause was that `_legacy_command_invokes_goal_stop_hook(...)` required at least three tokens and only validated Python launcher prefixes, discarding direct executable script hooks with two tokens. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now accept shell-form commands whose first token is `claude_goal.py` and whose second token is `stop-hook`. `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover relative and quoted direct shebang shell-form variants. Verification passed Git Bash shebang proof, manual before/after detector probes (`False False` to `True True`), focused doctor/installer tests (`1 passed`, `1 passed` after one invalid unquoted-spaced fixture was corrected), full nested suite (`385 passed`), Ruff format check, Ruff check, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check`, nested graph detect risk `0.00`, and `.tmp/pytest-t2078-*` cleanup. > Latest claude-goal hook detector env -S split-string fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. Reproduced that `/usr/bin/env -S "python -u" claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `env -S "python -u" claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and structured `env`/`/usr/bin/env` hooks with `["-S", "python -u", ...]` were not counted as `/goal` hooks even though GNU env `-S` executes the Python command. Verified execution with Git for Windows `C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\env.exe`; `env -S "python -u" ...` and `env -S "python -u -B" ...` wrote the marker script. Root cause was that env `-S` can carry the Python launcher and pre-script options inside one split-string token, while the detector only recognized already-tokenized launcher tokens. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now split the env `-S` command token, validate launcher/options, and preserve remaining pre-script options. `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover shell-form plus structured split-string variants. Verification passed actual `env.exe` temp-script proof, manual before/after detector probes (`False False` to `True True`), focused doctor/installer tests (`2 passed`, `1 passed`), full nested suite (`385 passed`), Ruff format check, Ruff check, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check`, nested graph detect risk `0.00`, and `.tmp/pytest-t2077-*` cleanup. > Latest claude-goal hook detector Python hash-pyc equals option fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. > Latest claude-goal hook detector Python stdin mode fix: 2026-06-10 Codex. Continued the user-requested `/goal` autonomous debugging loop in the separate `claude-goal/` subrepo while preserving existing nested WIP and concurrent root work. Reproduced that `python - "claude_goal.py" stop-hook` was counted as a `/goal` Stop hook even though exact `-` makes Python read code from stdin and pass `claude_goal.py` as an argument; temp-script proof showed `python -` did not execute the file while `python --` and `python -i` did. `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now reject exact `-` in pre-script Python options, with regression coverage in `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py`. Direct CLI QA showed several subcommands rejected a trailing bare `--` instead of accepting it as a standard option terminator: `contract --` fell through to raw argparse `unrecognized arguments: --`, `doctor --` returned `goal error: unknown doctor option: --`, and `status --` returned `goal error: status does not accept arguments: --`. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding `_strip_trailing_option_terminator()`, applying it to slash stale/flag-value parsers and parsed main argv, allowing no-arg slash commands to ignore exactly `--`, and preserving terminator semantics before trailing help rendering. Added direct and slash regression coverage in `tests/test_claude_goal.py` for `status`, `contract`, `doctor`, `json`, and `pause-stale`. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1861-option-terminator-qa.json`. > Latest claude-goal default UTF-8 captured output for Korean Windows paths: 2026-06-09 T-1858. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work. The local probe was relabeled from T-1857 because live shared context already assigned T-1857 to top-level help clear-alias grouping. Full `tests/test_claude_goal.py` without UTF-8 environment forcing reproduced 6 failures where captured stderr/stdout paths under the Korean Windows profile decoded as replacement characters; full `tests/` then reproduced the same captured-output issue in `install_goal.py` with 5 installer path-message failures. Root cause: both CLI entrypoints only set `errors=backslashreplace` on stdout/stderr while leaving the child output encoding on the Windows locale, but tests and CI-style captures decode as UTF-8. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` to reconfigure stdout/stderr with UTF-8 plus `backslashreplace` unless `PYTHONIOENCODING` is explicitly set, preserving intentional cp949 escape compatibility. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1858-output-encoding-qa.json`. Verification passed: focused `/goal` encoding/cp949 cluster (`23 passed, 155 deselected`), focused installer encoding/cp949 cluster (`6 passed, 113 deselected`), full `tests/test_claude_goal.py` without UTF-8 env forcing (`178 passed`), full `tests/` without UTF-8 env forcing (`350 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format-check, path-limited diff-check, graph detect risk `0.00`, and nested root check `NO_CLAUDE_GOAL_ROOT_CLAUDE`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal Stop hook subcommand adjacency guard: 2026-06-09 T-1855. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1854/T-1853/T-1852 fixes. Direct settings QA reproduced a distinct structured hook false-positive: a hook with command `python` and args `[claude_goal.py, status, stop-hook]` was removed by `install_settings()` during migration and was counted by `doctor --install --json` as exactly one configured `/goal` Stop hook, even though the actual `claude_goal.py` subcommand was `status`. Root cause: the T-1853 hook detector still accepted any later `stop-hook` token after a `claude_goal.py` script token. Fixed both `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` so structured and legacy hook commands require `stop-hook` to be the immediate token after the `claude_goal.py` script token; command-as-script hooks require `stop-hook` as the first arg. Extended install migration and doctor install-health regressions with the structured non-stop-hook command. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1855-hook-adjacency-qa.json`. Verification passed after RED: focused regressions, combined installer + install-script suite (`142 passed`), install-health cluster (`48 passed, 128 deselected`), py_compile, Ruff check/format-check after formatting, path-limited diff-check, graph detect risk `0.00`, and nested root check `NO_CLAUDE_GOAL_ROOT_CLAUDE`. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1852/T-1851/T-1850 fixes. > Latest claude-goal install_settings hook-config backup guard: 2026-06-09 T-1851. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1850/T-1849/T-1848 fixes. Direct API QA reproduced that `install_settings(settings_path, script_path, stop_block_cap="0")`, invalid `goal_max_stop_continues`, blank `python_executable`, or dash-prefixed `python_executable` raised `ValueError` but still created `settings.json.bak` when `settings.json` already existed and `create_backup=True`. Root cause: `install_settings()` created the backup before `build_stop_hook()` validated the hook command and cap values. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by building/validating the structured Stop hook before backup creation, then appending that prevalidated hook after backup and existing-hook cleanup. Added parameterized `tests/test_install_goal.py` direct-API coverage proving invalid hook config leaves `settings.json` unchanged and creates no backup. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1851-install-hook-config-qa.json`. Verification passed after RED: focused regression including T-1850 shape guard (`6 passed, 113 deselected`), combined installer + install-script suite (`142 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format-check, path-limited diff-check, graph detect risk `0.00`, and nested root check `NO_CLAUDE_GOAL_ROOT_CLAUDE`. > Latest claude-goal install_goal invalid settings-shape backup guard: 2026-06-09 T-1850. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1849/T-1848/T-1846 fixes. Direct installer QA reproduced that valid JSON with invalid settings shape (`{"hooks":"broken"}` or `{"hooks":{"Stop":"broken"}}`) made `goal/scripts/install_goal.py <settings> <goal/scripts/claude_goal.py>` exit `2` but still create `settings.json.bak`. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by moving backup creation after semantic settings-shape validation and before mutation/write. Added parameterized `tests/test_install_goal.py` coverage proving invalid shape leaves `settings.json` unchanged and creates no backup. > Latest claude-goal install.sh option terminator support: 2026-06-09 T-1849. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1848/T-1846/T-1845 fixes. Direct install-script QA reproduced that `HOME=<temp-home> ./install.sh --` exited `2` with `install.sh error: unknown option: --` instead of treating a bare option terminator as the end of options. Root cause: `install.sh` handled exact help and generic argument errors before recognizing `--`, so the terminator fell through to the unknown-option branch. Fixed `install.sh` by shifting a leading `--` before normal argument handling; bare `--` now installs normally, while arguments after `--` are treated as unexpected positional arguments. Added `tests/test_install_script.py` coverage for both bare `--` successful install and `-- --help` no-install error behavior. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1849-install-script-dashdash-qa.json`. Verification passed focused option-terminator regression after final relabel (`2 passed, 21 deselected`), full install script pytest after final relabel (`23 passed`), py_compile for `tests/test_install_script.py`, `bash -n install.sh`, Ruff check/format-check for `tests/test_install_script.py`, path-limited diff-check, graph detect risk `0.00`, and final root check `NO_ROOT_CLAUDE`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal install.sh invalid settings partial-install guard: 2026-06-09 T-1846. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1845/T-1844/T-1843 fixes. Direct install-script QA reproduced that when `HOME` points to an existing temp directory with broken `.claude/settings.json`, `./install.sh` exits `2` with `goal installer error: invalid JSON...` but had already created `.claude/skills/goal` as a symlink. Root cause: `install.sh` created install directories, replaced the skill symlink, applied chmod, and cleaned legacy command shims before delegating `settings.json` validation/update to `goal/scripts/install_goal.py`. Fixed `install.sh` by running the Python settings installer first, then creating install directories, symlink/chmod side effects, and legacy shim cleanup only after the settings install succeeds. Added `tests/test_install_script.py` coverage that prepares invalid settings, verifies exit `2`, verifies no success output, verifies no skill symlink, and confirms settings/backup/tmp files remain unchanged. > Latest claude-goal install.sh missing HOME path guard: 2026-06-09 T-1845. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1844/T-1843/T-1842 fixes. Direct install-script QA reproduced that when `HOME` points to a nonexistent path, `./install.sh` exits `0`, creates that fake HOME directory, and writes `.claude/settings.json` under it. Root cause: `install.sh` rejected empty HOME, filesystem-root HOME, and existing non-directory HOME paths, but did not require HOME itself to exist before `mkdir -p` created `$HOME/.claude/...`. Fixed `install.sh` by rejecting nonexistent HOME paths with exit `2` and `install.sh error: HOME must be an existing directory.` before any directory, link, or settings writes. Added `tests/test_install_script.py` coverage that runs with a missing HOME path, verifies the clear error, verifies raw `mkdir:` output is absent, and confirms the fake HOME path is not created. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1845-install-script-missing-home-qa.json`. Verification passed focused missing-HOME-path regression after formatting (`1 passed, 18 deselected`), full install script pytest after formatting (`19 passed`), py_compile for `tests/test_install_script.py`, `bash -n install.sh`, Ruff check/format-check for `tests/test_install_script.py`, path-limited diff-check, graph detect risk `0.00`, and final root check `NO_ROOT_CLAUDE`. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1843/T-1842/T-1841 fixes. Direct install-script QA reproduced that `./install.sh --help=false`, `./install.sh -h=false`, and `./install.sh -hfoo` exited `2` without installing but reported `unknown option` instead of the clearer help-value error used by the Python installer surface. > Latest claude-goal install.sh empty HOME guard: 2026-06-09 T-1838. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1837/T-1836 fixes. Direct shell QA reproduced that `HOME= ./install.sh` exited successfully and installed into WSL root `/.claude`: output included `Updated Stop hook in /.claude/settings.json`, `Skill: /.claude/skills/goal`, and the probe confirmed `ROOT_CLAUDE_CREATED`. Root cause: `install.sh` used `$HOME/.claude/...` paths without validating that `HOME` was set to a non-empty value, so empty `HOME` expanded the install target to `/.claude`. Fixed `install.sh` by checking `if [ -z "${HOME:-}" ]` before any writes and returning exit `2` with `install.sh error: HOME must be set before installing.` Added `tests/test_install_script.py` coverage for both `HOME= ./install.sh` and `env -u HOME ./install.sh`, including no-root-creation verification when `/.claude` was absent before the run. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1838-install-script-empty-home-qa.json`. Verification passed focused missing-HOME regression (`2 passed, 7 deselected`), full install script pytest after formatting (`9 passed`), py_compile for `tests/test_install_script.py`, `bash -n install.sh`, Ruff check/format-check for `tests/test_install_script.py`, path-limited diff-check, and graph detect risk `0.00`. The repro-created WSL `/.claude` artifact was removed after marker checks and final root check reported `NO_ROOT_CLAUDE`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal install.sh existing skill directory replacement fix: 2026-06-09 T-1837. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1836/T-1835 fixes. Direct shell QA reproduced that when `$HOME/.claude/skills/goal` already exists as a real directory, `./install.sh` exits `0` but leaves stale contents and creates a nested `skills/goal/goal -> <repo>/goal` symlink instead of replacing `skills/goal` itself. Root cause: `ln -sfn "$ROOT/goal" "$HOME/.claude/skills/goal"` treats an existing non-symlink directory destination as a directory target. Fixed `install.sh` by assigning `SKILL_PATH`, removing that owned path only when it exists and is not already a symlink, then creating the skill symlink. Added `tests/test_install_script.py` coverage that prepares an existing stale skill directory and verifies with bash `test -L` that final `skills/goal` is the symlink, stale content is gone, and no nested `goal` symlink remains. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1837-install-script-existing-skill-dir-qa.json`. Verification passed focused regression (`1 passed, 6 deselected`), full install script pytest (`7 passed`), py_compile for `tests/test_install_script.py`, `bash -n install.sh`, Ruff check/format-check for `tests/test_install_script.py`, path-limited diff-check, and graph detect risk `0.00`. A mistaken Ruff run on `install.sh` was corrected with `bash -n`; an accidental WSL `/.claude` repro artifact was removed after marker checks. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal raw slash apostrophe subcommand parsing fix: 2026-06-09 T-1836. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1835/T-1834 fixes. Direct CLI QA reproduced that direct argv paths treat apostrophes as literal user text, while raw slash `invoke` leaked `goal error: No closing quotation`: direct `help customer's` returned `unknown help topic: customer's`, but `invoke "help customer's"` returned `No closing quotation`; raw slash `json --session-id customer's-session` and `contract customer's --help` shared the same parser boundary. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding `_split_raw_slash_tokens()`, which keeps normal `shlex.split()` behavior for balanced quoting but falls back to literal whitespace tokenization only for `No closing quotation`, then reused it in raw invoke/help/subcommand and flag/value parsers. Added `tests/test_claude_goal.py` regression coverage for apostrophe-bearing raw help topics, `json --session-id` values, and invalid contract trailing-help input. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1836-raw-invoke-apostrophe-qa.json`; A/B adopted `.tmp/ab-manifest-t1836-raw-invoke-apostrophe.json` with result `.tmp/ab-result-t1836-raw-invoke-apostrophe.json` (`score_delta=2.142857142857143`, failed gates `[]`). Verification passed focused regression (`1 passed`), help/json cluster (`8 passed, 167 deselected`), `tests/test_claude_goal.py` (`175 passed in 242.58s`), full `tests/` (`321 passed in 248.59s`), py_compile, Ruff check/format, path-limited diff-check, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1831/T-1832/T-1833/T-1834 fixes. Documentation/runtime QA reproduced that `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY='' contract --json-output <tmp>/release-evidence/goal-operating-contract.json --markdown-output <tmp>/release-evidence/goal-operating-contract.md --github-step-summary` and `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY='' doctor --install --json --github-step-summary` both exit `2` with `goal error: --github-step-summary requires GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY to be set`, while README/SKILL documented only that `--github-step-summary` appends to `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` and omitted the required env-file precondition plus local fallback. Fixed `README.md` and `goal/SKILL.md` to state that `--github-step-summary` requires `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`, GitHub Actions sets it automatically for workflow steps, and local/non-Actions scripts should use `--markdown-output <path>`. Updated `tests/test_readme.py` and `tests/test_skill_docs.py` to lock the wording. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1835-github-step-summary-doc-precondition-qa.json`. > Latest claude-goal trailing slash subcommand help parity fix: 2026-06-09 T-1834. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and T-1831/T-1832/T-1833 docs/help fixes. Direct CLI QA reproduced that direct commands such as `json --session-id abc --help`, `pause-stale --yes --help`, and `complete --schema --help` render normal argparse help, while slash `invoke` treated the same trailing help flags as unknown options (`unknown json option: --help`, `unknown pause-stale option: --help`, `unknown complete option: --help`). > Latest claude-goal set option-terminator literal handling fix: 2026-06-09 T-1827. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent root auto-research relay work and earlier T-1825/T-1826 quote-objective fixes. Direct CLI QA reproduced that the custom `set` parser ignored the conventional `--` end-of-options boundary: `set -- --help` rendered set help and created no objective, `set objective -- --tokens 10` created objective `objective --` while incorrectly parsing token budget `10`, and slash fallback `invoke "objective -- --budget 10"` shared the same parser defect. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by splitting `set` tokens at the first `--`, parsing only pre-terminator tokens as `set` options/help, and appending post-terminator tokens as literal objective text; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_set_option_terminator_keeps_following_tokens_as_objective_text` for direct/slash paths. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1827-set-option-terminator-qa.json`; A/B adopted `.tmp/ab-manifest-t1827-set-option-terminator.json` with result `.tmp/ab-result-t1827-set-option-terminator.json` (`score_delta=2.666667`). Verification passed direct after-QA, focused regression (`1 passed`), related help/set parser pytest (`5 passed, 166 deselected`), `tests/test_claude_goal.py` on current combined T-1826/T-1827 state (`172 passed in 224.44s`), full `tests/` (`318 passed in 240.75s`), py_compile, Ruff check/format, path-limited diff-check, and graph detect risk `0.00`. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent T-1811/T-1810/T-1809 relays and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. > Latest claude-goal installer python-executable dash-value fix: 2026-06-09 T-1811. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent T-1810/T-1809/T-1808 relays and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated installer QA showed `--python-executable=-1`, `--python-executable=-.5`, and `--python-executable=-` exited `0` and wrote Stop hook commands such as `-1` or `-`, creating unusable installed hooks; split forms like `--python-executable -1` behaved the same. Cap options still needed to preserve negative numeric values for positive-integer validation, so this was specific to the executable command field. Root cause: `normalize_python_executable()` only rejected blank executable values, while installer prevalidation intentionally let negative numeric-looking tokens through for cap validation. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` so normalized python executable values starting with `-` raise `python_executable must not start with '-'` before settings writes. Added direct API and CLI regression coverage in `tests/test_install_goal.py` for split/inline dash-leading executable values. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1811-installer-python-executable-dash-value-qa.json` confirms six split/inline dash-leading executable cases exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `python_executable must not start with '-'`, avoid `expected one argument`, and create no settings file; four negative cap controls still report `must be a positive integer`, and a normal `--python-executable python3` install still writes settings. Verification passed focused pytest (`34 passed, 73 deselected`), full installer module (`107 passed`), long `tests/test_claude_goal.py` with `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`160 passed in 212.39s`), remaining five test files (`36 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal installer single-dash value-option fix: 2026-06-09 T-1809. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent T-1808/T-1807/T-1806 relays and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated installer QA showed value options still mishandled single-dash option-looking values after the T-1806 `--...` fix: split `--python-executable -bogus` and `--stop-block-cap -bogus` exited `2` with argparse `expected one argument`, inline `--python-executable=-bogus` exited `0` and wrote a Stop hook command of `-bogus`, and inline cap forms such as `--stop-block-cap=-bogus` reported `must be a positive integer` instead of the missing-value boundary. Root cause: `prevalidate_option_values()` only rejected `--...` option-looking values, so `-bogus` fell through to argparse or later normalizers. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by adding installer-local option-like value detection that rejects single-dash nonnumeric option-looking values while preserving negative numeric cap controls for positive-integer validation. Extended `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_cli_reports_parser_errors_without_argparse_usage` for split/inline `-bogus` values across `--python-executable`, `--stop-block-cap`, and `--max-stop-continues`. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1809-installer-single-dash-option-value-qa.json` confirms six split/inline `-bogus` value cases exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `--option requires a value`, avoid `expected one argument` and positive-integer masking, and create no settings file; four `-1` cap controls still report `must be a positive integer`, and a normal `--python-executable python3` install still writes settings. Verification passed focused parser pytest (`27 passed, 73 deselected`), full installer module (`100 passed`), long `tests/test_claude_goal.py` with `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`160 passed in 202.53s`), remaining five test files (`36 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. > Latest claude-goal CLI help-value feedback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1805. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent T-1804/T-1803/T-1802/T-1801 relays and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated CLI QA showed invalid help-with-value/extra forms were inconsistent across the main `/goal` command surface: `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py set --help=false` and `invoke --help=false` exited `0` and created active goals with objective `--help=false`; root, direct-subcommand, and slash-subcommand split forms such as `--help false`, `status --help false`, and `invoke complete --help false` exited `0` with help while silently ignoring the extra token. Root cause: help validation covered exact `-h/--help` in several paths, but inline help values and help-plus-extra tokens could bypass validation through legacy `set`/`invoke` dispatch, direct main argparse help, or slash subcommand help routing. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by centralizing help option metadata, rejecting inline `-h/--help=value`, rejecting split `-h/--help <extra>` before argparse can mask it, applying the guard to legacy set/invoke fallback and direct/slash subcommand help rendering, and preserving plain help. Updated `tests/test_claude_goal.py` so plain help stays non-mutating while help values/extras return exit `2` without creating goals. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1805-help-value-feedback-qa.json` confirms 18 invalid cases exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `goal error: -h/--help does not accept a value`, create no goal rows, avoid argparse jargon, and print no traceback; 5 plain help controls still exit `0` with usage and no state writes. Verification passed focused help pytest (`4 passed, 155 deselected`), single first-test isolation, long `tests/test_claude_goal.py` clean rerun (`159 passed`), final full isolated `tests` (`289 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal installer abbreviated-option strictness fix: 2026-06-09 T-1804. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent T-1803/T-1802/T-1801 relays and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated installer QA showed undocumented option forms were accepted: `-hfoo`, `--he`, and post-positional `-hfoo` exited `0` with help, while `--py python3 <settings> <script>` abbreviated `--python-executable`, exited `0`, and wrote `settings.json`. Root cause: installer prevalidation only handled exact documented help/value/boolean tokens, while argparse's default parsing still allowed long-option abbreviation and short help attached values. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by constructing `InstallerArgumentParser(..., allow_abbrev=False)` and rejecting `-h...` attached values before argparse. Added regression coverage in `tests/test_install_goal.py` for short attached help values and abbreviated long options without installation. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1804-installer-abbreviated-options-qa.json` confirms `-hfoo`, `--he`, `--py python3 <settings> <script>`, and `<settings> <script> --no` now exit `2`, leave stdout empty, and create no settings file, while plain `--help` still exits `0`. Verification passed focused parser pytest (`15 passed`), full installer module (`88 passed`), split full-suite coverage (`tests/test_claude_goal.py`: `159 passed in 203.46s`; remaining six test files: `124 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. The first `tests/test_claude_goal.py` run hit Windows SQLite file-lock setup errors from its own orphaned pytest/subprocess tree; only those verification PIDs were stopped and the fresh-basetemp rerun passed. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal installer help-option-extra feedback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1803. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrent T-1802/T-1801 relays and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated installer QA showed `python goal/scripts/install_goal.py --help --no-backup` and post-positional `settings script --help --no-backup` exited `0`, printed help, and silently ignored the extra option. This was inconsistent with T-1802, which already rejects help values such as `--help false`. Root cause: `prevalidate_option_values()` rejected values after help only when the next token was non-option text, allowing option-looking extras to reach argparse's eager help action. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` so `-h/--help` rejects any following token before argparse can mask it. Extended `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_cli_help_options_reject_values_without_argparse_jargon` with leading, short, and post-positional help-option-extra cases. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1803-installer-help-option-extra-qa.json` confirms `--help --no-backup`, `-h --restore-backup`, and `settings script --help --no-backup` exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `goal installer error: -h/--help does not accept a value`, create no settings file, and plain `--help` still exits `0`. Verification passed focused help-option pytest (`10 passed`), full installer module (`83 passed`), split full-suite coverage (`tests/test_claude_goal.py`: `158 passed in 188.17s`; remaining six test files: `119 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. A PowerShell `Start-Process -ArgumentList` probe initially misquoted the workspace path with spaces; the final QA artifact was regenerated with Python subprocess capture. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal installer help-value feedback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1802. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus latest concurrent T-1801 install.sh relay and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated installer QA showed `python goal/scripts/install_goal.py --help=false`, `-h=false`, `--help false`, `-h false`, and post-positional help-with-value forms had inconsistent UX: inline forms exited `2` but leaked argparse jargon `argument -h/--help: ignored explicit argument`, while split forms exited `0`, printed help, and silently ignored the extra value. Root cause: `prevalidate_option_values()` treated `-h/--help` as known options for value-option missing-value checks, but did not validate help as a value-free option before argparse's help action ran. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by adding `INSTALLER_HELP_OPTIONS` and rejecting inline/split values for `-h` and `--help` before argparse. Added `tests/test_install_goal.py` coverage for help value rejection plus a control proving plain `--help` still prints help without installing. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1802-installer-help-value-feedback-qa.json` confirms all six help-with-value cases exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `goal installer error: -h/--help does not accept a value`, avoid `ignored explicit argument`, avoid help masking/usage output, create no settings file, and print no traceback; plain `--help` and `-h` still exit `0` with installer help and no settings writes. Verification passed focused help/parser pytest (`22 passed, 58 deselected`), full installer module (`80 passed`), full isolated pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`274 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal install.sh help-extra argument validation fix: 2026-06-09 T-1801. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated shell QA showed `./install.sh --help extra` and `./install.sh extra --help` exited `0`, printed help, and skipped install even though usage is `./install.sh [--help|-h]`. Root cause: `install.sh` scanned all arguments for help before validating arity or unexpected arguments, so a help token anywhere masked invalid extra input. Fixed `install.sh` so help exits `0` only when it is the sole argument; help with extras and unexpected positional arguments now exit `2` without installing. Added `tests/test_install_script.py::test_install_script_help_with_extra_args_exits_without_installing`. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1801-install-sh-help-extra-qa.json` confirms `--help extra` and `extra --help` exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report the specific install.sh error plus usage hint, create no settings/skill files, and plain `--help` still exits `0` without installing. Verification passed focused shell pytest (`6 passed`), related installer tests (`73 passed`), split full-suite coverage (`tests/test_claude_goal.py`: `158 passed in 198.14s`; remaining six test files: `116 passed`), `bash -n install.sh`, py_compile, Ruff check/format for the touched test, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. Single monolithic pytest attempts hit the local command runtime boundary around the long `tests/test_claude_goal.py` file, so the final full verification was intentionally chunked. No nested code staging, push, revert, root product/wiki edit, browser UI change, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal installer empty value feedback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1800. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated installer QA showed value options with explicit empty values were blocked before side effects but reported inconsistent diagnostics: `--python-executable=` / split empty `--python-executable ""` reported `python_executable must not be blank`, while empty `--stop-block-cap` / `--max-stop-continues` forms reported `must be a positive integer`. Root cause: `prevalidate_option_values()` only rejected end-of-argv, `--`, or known option tokens after value flags, so blank inline/split values fell through to later normalizers. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` so blank inline and split values for installer value flags raise `--option requires a value` before argparse or settings writes. Updated `tests/test_install_goal.py` parser-feedback coverage and CLI blank-python-executable expectation. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1800-installer-empty-value-feedback-qa.json` confirms all six empty value cases exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `goal installer error: --option requires a value`, avoid stale blank/integer diagnostics, create no settings file, and print no traceback/usage. Verification passed focused parser/blank/invalid-cap pytest (`19 passed, 54 deselected`), full installer module (`73 passed`), full isolated pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`267 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. > Latest claude-goal complete help required-audit copy fix: 2026-06-09 T-1796. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrently recorded T-1795/T-1794 and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct CLI reproduction after T-1794 showed `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py complete --help` still described completion as happening "preferably after verifying a completion audit artifact", while plain `complete` now rejects no-audit mutation. Root cause: `_add_complete_parser()` help text was not updated when `--audit-json` became mandatory for mutating completion. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` so the command description says completion happens after verifying a completion audit artifact and the `--audit-json` option says it is required unless `--schema` is used. > Latest claude-goal installer boolean flag value feedback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1795. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus concurrently recorded T-1794/T-1793 and root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated CLI QA showed installer boolean flags with accidental values were blocked safely but reported generic argparse jargon: split values (`--no-backup false`, `--restore-backup false`, `--yes false`) produced `unrecognized arguments: false`, and inline values (`--no-backup=false`, `--restore-backup=false`, `--yes=false`) produced `ignored explicit argument`. Root cause: the installer wrapped argparse errors but did not prevalidate known boolean flags before argparse parsed following/inline values. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` with `INSTALLER_BOOLEAN_OPTIONS` and `prevalidate_boolean_option_values()` before `parse_args()`; added `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_cli_boolean_options_reject_values_without_argparse_jargon`. Verification passed after-fix probes, focused parser pytest (`14 passed`), full installer module (`61 passed`), full isolated pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`253 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check/format for touched files, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. > Latest claude-goal complete requires completion audit fix: 2026-06-09 T-1794. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus T-1793/T-1792 and concurrent root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated CLI reproduction showed a goal could be mutated to `complete` with plain `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py complete`, without a `--audit-json` completion audit. Root cause: `_render_complete_command()` only verified an audit artifact when `complete_args.audit_json` was present, and `_validate_complete_args()` allowed no-audit mutation. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` so `complete` requires `--audit-json` unless `--schema` is being requested. Updated `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_set_status_pause_resume_complete` to complete with an audit artifact and added `test_complete_requires_audit_json_without_mutating` for direct and slash invoke paths. > Latest claude-goal installer settings-parent feedback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1792. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus T-1790/T-1789 and concurrent root auto-research dirty-handoff work. Direct isolated reproduction showed an existing file as the parent of `settings.json` made `python goal/scripts/install_goal.py <settings-parent-file>/settings.json <script>` exit `2` without traceback but report a raw localized `WinError 183` instead of naming the invalid parent path. Root cause: `validate_settings_write_path()` checked the settings file and temp path, but did not validate that the parent was a directory before `write_settings()` called `mkdir()`. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` with `validate_parent_directory_path()` and invoked it from settings write preflight; added `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_cli_rejects_settings_parent_file_without_raw_os_error`. > Latest claude-goal doctor schema runtime-flag conflict fix: 2026-06-09 T-1784. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus recorded T-1783 doctor verify-json stale-days conflict, T-1782 install.sh argument-boundary, and T-1781 completion-audit objective preflight fixes. Root cause: `_validate_doctor_schema_conflicts()` rejected output-mode and output-sink conflicts but did not receive or check `strict` or explicit `--stale-days`, so those runtime flags were silently ignored in schema mode. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by extending `_validate_doctor_schema_conflicts()` with `strict` and `stale_days_explicit` and rejecting them as `doctor --schema` conflicts; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_doctor_schema_rejects_runtime_flags_before_db_open` for direct and slash paths with a bad DB path. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1784-doctor-schema-runtime-flags-qa.json` confirms direct/slash `--strict` conflicts now return `goal error: doctor --schema cannot be combined with --strict`, direct/slash explicit `--stale-days` conflicts return `goal error: doctor --schema cannot be combined with --stale-days`, DB-open masking is absent, and plain direct/slash schema output still returns JSON. > Latest claude-goal doctor verify-json stale-days conflict fix: 2026-06-09 T-1783. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus recorded T-1782 install.sh argument-boundary, T-1781 completion-audit objective preflight, and T-1780 install output quoting fixes. With a missing artifact and `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB` pointing at a directory, both reported `cannot read install-health JSON artifact` instead of rejecting the meaningless verify-mode option before artifact reads and DB work. > Latest claude-goal install.sh help/unknown-argument side-effect boundary fix: 2026-06-09 T-1782. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify UX loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus recorded T-1781 completion-audit objective preflight, T-1780 install output quoting, and T-1779 doctor strict install-required fixes. Direct isolated `install.sh` probes with repo-local temp `HOME` showed `./install.sh --help` and `./install.sh --dry-run` ignored their arguments, performed normal install, wrote `.claude/settings.json`, and created `.claude/skills/goal`. Classification: functional error, loading/feedback, and copy. External reference checked: GNU command-line standards say `--help` should print invocation help and not perform the normal command function. Root cause: `install.sh` had no argument parser, so every invocation flowed straight into side-effecting install work. Fixed `install.sh` by adding `usage()`, early `-h|--help` handling with exit `0`, and explicit unknown-option/unexpected-argument errors with exit `2` before any install directories/files are created; updated `tests/test_install_script.py` with actual Bash execution tests that set repo-local `.tmp` `HOME` and assert help/unknown cases create no settings file or skill symlink while normal install still works. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1782-install-sh-arg-boundary-qa.json` records `adopt_candidate` and confirms `--help` exits `0`, prints usage on stdout, leaves stderr empty, and creates no side-effect files; `--dry-run` exits `2`, leaves stdout empty, reports `install.sh error: unknown option: --dry-run` plus the help hint, and creates no side-effect files; and normal no-arg install still returns `0`, prints `Installed /goal for Claude Code.`, and creates settings plus skill symlink. Verification passed focused install script pytest (`4 passed`), `bash -n install.sh`, full isolated pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`233 passed`), py_compile, scoped Ruff check/format for `tests/test_install_script.py`, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.
