> Latest claude-goal install.sh copy-paste command quoting fix: 2026-06-09 T-1780. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus recorded T-1779 doctor strict install-required, T-1778 restore-mode installer boundary, and T-1777 parser-error boundary fixes. Direct isolated `install.sh` reproduction in the Windows/WSL workspace showed printed Stop hook, doctor, and restore commands used an unescaped repo path containing the space in `Vibe coding`, so copying the displayed `python3 .../Vibe coding/...` command into Bash would split the script path before reaching `doctor --install` or restore mode. Root cause: actual installer invocation was already quoted, but the user-facing `echo` lines interpolated raw `$ROOT` and `$HOME` paths. Fixed `install.sh` by adding a Bash `shell_quote()` helper using `printf '%q'`, precomputing printable script/settings paths, and using those values only for displayed copy-paste commands; updated `tests/test_install_script.py` to lock shell-quoted display variables and absence of raw unescaped doctor/restore command strings. QA artifact `.tmp/claude-goal-t1780-install-sh-quoted-output-qa.json` records `adopt_candidate` and confirms actual output escapes `Vibe coding` as `Vibe\ coding` for Stop hook, doctor, and restore copy commands; raw unescaped doctor/restore lines are absent; the displayed doctor script path executes and returns parseable install-health JSON; and stderr stays empty. Verification passed focused install script pytest (`2 passed`), `bash -n install.sh`, full isolated pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`230 passed`), py_compile, scoped Ruff check/format for `tests/test_install_script.py`, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. A full pytest attempt without exported `PYTHONUTF8` failed only on existing Korean Windows child-subprocess path mojibake assertions (`222 passed, 8 failed`) before the UTF-8 rerun passed. Broader `ruff format --check goal tests` currently reports existing dirty `tests/test_claude_goal.py` format drift, so it was not reformatted during this scoped T-1780 change. No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP plus recorded T-1777 parser-error boundary, T-1776 invalid JSON path feedback, and T-1775 installer type-validation usage fixes. Direct isolated reproduction showed restore mode silently accepted install-only inputs: positional `script_path`, `--no-backup`, `--python-executable`, `--stop-block-cap`, and `--max-stop-continues` all returned exit `0` with `--restore-backup`, and `--restore-backup --yes` could apply the restore while misleading install-only args were present. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by leaving install-only option defaults as `None`, rejecting explicit install-only inputs when `--restore-backup` is active, and applying python/cap defaults and validation only in install mode; added CLI and direct `parse_args()` regression coverage in `tests/test_install_goal.py`. Verification passed focused restore/parser pytest (`12 passed`), installer pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`40 passed`; non-UTF8 attempt failed only on existing Korean-path subprocess decoding assertions), full isolated pytest with exported `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`230 passed`), py_compile, Ruff check, scoped Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. Root cause: `_prevalidate_main_required_values()` returned early for unknown top-level command names, leaving argparse to format the failure before the app-level `goal error` boundary. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding a consolidated `MAIN_COMMANDS` set and rejecting unknown top-level direct commands/options before argparse, while preserving no-arg help, `--help`/`-h`, and `set`/`invoke` objective fallback behavior; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_direct_unknown_commands_reject_before_argparse_usage`. > Latest claude-goal quoted invoke objective token-option parse fix: 2026-06-09 T-1756. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing WIP and the completed T-1755 direct value-option missing-value surface. Direct isolated CLI reproduction showed `invoke "objective text" --tokens 10`, `invoke "objective text" --tokens=10`, and quoted-objective invalid/missing token-budget variants failed before set fallback parsing with `goal error: No closing quotation`; the same objective without options worked, and unquoted word-by-word objective plus token options worked. Root cause: `handle_main_invoke()` round-trips multi-argv input through `shell_command(*args.args)`, which correctly shell-quotes an argv containing spaces, but `_parse_invoke_command()` split that shell-escaped string with plain `.split(maxsplit=1)`, breaking the opening quote before `parse_set_args()` could use `shlex.split()`. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by making `_parse_invoke_command()` parse command boundaries with `shlex.split()` and rebuild `rest` with `shell_command(*parts[1:])`; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_quoted_objective_preserves_token_options`. After-fix QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1756-quoted-invoke-token-qa.json` records score `4/4`, `adopt_candidate`, and confirms quoted-objective valid split/inline token-budget forms set the intended objective and budget, while invalid/missing values return the specific token-budget usage errors without `No closing quotation` or traceback. > Latest claude-goal stale-days empty-value guard fix: 2026-06-09 T-1754. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` computer-use reproduce -> research -> A/B -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing WIP and the concurrently recorded T-1753 direct JSON empty session-id parity surface. Direct CLI reproduction showed `doctor --stale-days= --json`, `pause-stale --stale-days= --json`, and split-empty `--stale-days ""` variants produced inconsistent empty-value feedback: direct commands exposed argparse `invalid int value: ''`, while slash `invoke` paths classified the same empty value as `--stale-days must be an integer`. The correct contract is a missing-value usage error before DB open. Root cause: `_parse_stale_days_value()` called `int(value)` without first validating blank values, and direct `doctor`/`pause-stale` argparse definitions used `type=int`, so empty strings were consumed before shared option validation. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by routing direct stale-days values through `_parse_stale_days_value()`, validating blank values with `_validate_option_value("--stale-days", ...)`, and removing direct argparse `type=int` for the affected options. Updated `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_stale_days_rejects_non_integer_before_db_open` and added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_stale_days_rejects_empty_values_before_db_open`. After-fix QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1753-stale-days-empty-value-qa/summary.json` records score `8/8`, `adopt_candidate`, and confirms direct plus slash `doctor`/`pause-stale` empty `--stale-days` flows return exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `goal error: --stale-days requires a value`, avoid argparse `invalid int value`, avoid integer-format errors, avoid DB errors, and print no traceback. Verification passed focused stale-days pytest (`6 passed, 129 deselected`), command-cluster pytest (`67 passed, 68 deselected`), full isolated pytest with `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`194 passed`), Ruff check, scoped Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing WIP and the completed T-1750 empty audit-json surface. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing WIP and the completed T-1747b/T-1748 surfaces. 0-step refresh prioritized a structured-output data-integrity bug over lower-impact message-order differences: direct isolated DB reproduction showed `goals.metadata_json = '{broken'` made `json`/`contract` return exit `2` with raw JSON parser text and no `metadata_json` context, while `metadata_json = '[]'` or `"string"` made `json` and `contract` return exit `0` with schema-invalid `metadata` array/string values; `contract --verify-json` accepted those artifacts when the same corrupt DB value was present. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after the completed T-1744 installer malformed Stop entry count fix. Direct isolated reproduction showed direct `set objective --token-budget nope` with `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB` pointing at a directory returned the actionable input error, and slash `invoke objective --token-budget nope` returned that clean error when the DB could open; however, slash `invoke objective --token-budget nope` and `invoke objective --token-budget` with `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB` pointing at an existing directory returned `cannot open goal database` before validating bad set fallback arguments. Root cause: slash `invoke` parsed objective-setting fallback arguments only inside `_render_invoke_db_command()` after `sqlite_connect()`. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding a DB-free set fallback preflight in `invoke()` before opening SQLite; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_set_rejects_invalid_token_args_before_db_open`. A/B decision in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1741-invoke-set-preflight-qa.json`: baseline masked input errors behind DB open failure; candidate returns actionable input errors first, so adopted. Verification passed focused regression pytest (`1 passed`), invoke/set cluster (`5 passed, 117 deselected`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, graph detect risk `0.00`, and full pytest under `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`175 passed`). No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal invoke pause-stale argument preflight fix: 2026-06-09 T-1740. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after the already-recorded T-1739b missing schema-docs fix. > Latest claude-goal missing schema docs no-traceback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1739b. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after the already-recorded T-1738 invoke-json preflight fix and avoided a pre-existing nested T-1739 pause-stale artifact. Reproduced by copying `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` into a temp install layout without `goal/docs/`: direct and slash `complete --schema`, `contract --schema`, and `doctor --install --schema` returned exit `1` with `FileNotFoundError` tracebacks. Root cause: schema renderers used raw `Path.read_text()` instead of the existing `_read_utf8_text_file()` user-error boundary. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` to wrap missing/unreadable schema files as `ValueError`; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_schema_commands_report_missing_schema_without_traceback`; hardened `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` subprocess decoding with UTF-8/error replacement for Korean Windows paths. After-fix QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1739b-schema-missing-docs-qa.json` confirms all six missing-docs schema cases return exit `2`, empty stdout, `goal error: cannot read <schema>`, and no traceback. Verification passed focused regression (`1 passed`), schema cluster (`5 passed`), cp949 helper regression (`1 passed`), full `tests/test_claude_goal.py` (`120 passed`), installer decoding focused tests (`3 passed`), final full isolated pytest (`174 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal Stop-hook non-string hook payload field fix: 2026-06-09 T-1737b. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1736, using suffix fallback after finding an existing `.tmp/claude-goal-t1737-complete-audit-preflight-qa.json` artifact for a distinct complete-audit preflight loop. Direct isolated Stop-hook reproduction with an active goal showed object payloads with malformed internal field types still crashed: `{"cwd":123}`, `{"cwd":["x"]}`, and `{"cwd":{"path":"x"}}` returned exit `1` with `AttributeError: '<type>' object has no attribute 'encode'`; `{"session_id":["t1737-session"]}` and `{"session_id":{"id":"t1737-session"}}` returned exit `1` with `sqlite3.ProgrammingError` while binding the session candidate. Root cause: `candidate_session_ids()` trusted `hook_data.session_id` and `hook_data.cwd` as strings; `cwd_session_id()` hashes `.encode()` and `get_goal()` binds session candidates into SQLite. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by normalizing hook payload fields through `_hook_string()` before deriving candidate session IDs; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_stop_hook_ignores_non_string_hook_payload_fields_without_traceback`. Verification passed direct after-fix CLI QA in workspace `.tmp/claude-goal-t1737b-stop-hook-non-string-fields-qa.json`, focused Stop-hook pytest (`6 passed, 111 deselected`), full isolated pytest (`170 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal Stop-hook non-object JSON payload fix: 2026-06-09 T-1736. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1735/T-1735b. Direct isolated Stop-hook reproduction with an active goal showed `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py stop-hook` tolerated empty stdin, malformed JSON, `{}`, `[]`, and `null`, but valid non-object JSON payloads such as `"hello"` and `123` returned exit `1` with `AttributeError: '<type>' object has no attribute 'get'` and a Python traceback; a non-empty array has the same root cause. Root cause: `_read_stop_hook_data()` caught JSON decode failures but returned any parsed JSON value, while `candidate_session_ids()` assumes dict-like hook data and calls `.get()`. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by normalizing parsed Stop-hook stdin to `{}` unless it is a JSON object; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_stop_hook_ignores_non_object_json_payload_without_traceback`. Verification passed direct after-fix CLI QA in workspace `.tmp/claude-goal-t1736-stop-hook-non-object-payload-qa.json`, focused Stop-hook pytest (`5 passed, 111 deselected`), full isolated pytest (`169 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. > Latest claude-goal installer blank python executable fix: 2026-06-09 T-1735. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1734. Direct isolated installer reproduction showed `python goal/scripts/install_goal.py <settings> <script> --no-backup --python-executable "   "` returned exit `0`, wrote `settings.json`, and installed a structured Stop hook with `"command": "   "`. Running `doctor --install --json` against that settings file immediately reported overall `fail` with `goal_stop_hook_command: command does not resolve:    `. Empty-string `--python-executable ""` fell back to `sys.executable`, so the confirmed bug is whitespace-only values. Root cause: the installer validated Stop-hook cap arguments but treated explicit `python_executable` as trusted text; whitespace-only strings are truthy and were written directly as the hook command. Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by normalizing explicit Python executable values with `strip()` and rejecting blank results through both argparse and `build_stop_hook()`/`install_settings()`; added `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_settings_rejects_blank_python_executable_without_writing` and `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_cli_rejects_blank_python_executable_without_writing`. Verification passed direct after-fix CLI QA in workspace `.tmp/claude-goal-t1735-installer-blank-python-executable-qa.json`, focused installer pytest (`5 passed, 16 deselected`), full isolated pytest (`167 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. This loop's temp dirs were removed. No stage, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal static schema DB-boundary fix: 2026-06-09 T-1734. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1733/T-1733b. Direct isolated reproduction with `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB` pointing at an existing directory showed static schema commands that should not need goal state failed before rendering schema: `complete --schema`, `invoke complete --schema`, and `invoke contract --schema` returned exit `2` with `goal error: cannot open goal database: ... unable to open database file`. Direct `contract --schema`, direct `doctor --install --schema`, and `invoke doctor --install --schema` already worked, proving the issue was specific to direct complete and slash complete/contract routing. Root cause: direct `handle_main_complete()` and slash `invoke()` entered `sqlite_connect()` before checking schema-only paths. > Latest claude-goal installer cp949 Unicode output fix: 2026-06-09 T-1733b. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after a concurrent relay already used `T-1733` for invoke-operation commands, so this loop uses the suffix fallback. Direct isolated installer reproduction with `PYTHONIOENCODING=cp949` and a `settings_path` containing U+2014 showed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` wrote `settings.json`, then returned exit `1` with a `UnicodeEncodeError` traceback while printing `Updated Stop hook in ...`. > Latest claude-goal slash operation-command parity fix: 2026-06-09 T-1733. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP. Direct isolated CLI reproduction showed direct `json` and `stop-hook` were safe operation commands, but slash `invoke json`, `invoke json extra`, `invoke stop-hook`, and `invoke stop-hook extra` fell through to `set_goal()` and created accidental objectives. Root cause: slash invoke dispatch treated operation commands without explicit handlers as objective text; `json` had no invoke route and `stop-hook` was not covered by the no-extra-args guard. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding explicit invoke routing for `json` and `stop-hook`, argument validation, and empty-output-safe printing for main and legacy invoke; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_json_matches_direct_without_mutating` and `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_stop_hook_matches_direct_without_setting_goal`. Verification passed direct after-fix QA in workspace `.tmp/claude-goal-t1733-invoke-operation-commands-qa.json`, focused pytest (`3 passed, 111 deselected`), order-dependency diagnosis (`3 passed, 111 deselected`), `doctor_install` group (`44 passed, 70 deselected`), full `tests/test_claude_goal.py` (`114 passed`), final full isolated pytest (`165 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest auto-research debug-loop inventory fix: 2026-06-09. Root loop found a deterministic evidence bug where `debug_loop_inventory.py` could label a clean `blocked_publish_only/current_head_release_checks_unproven` state as a dirty handoff blocker and could keep a Blind-to-X dirty-path blocker even when Blind-to-X had `dirty_paths=[]`. Fixed `.agents/skills/auto-research/scripts/debug_loop_inventory.py` to classify the primary item from selector kind plus dirty count, report publish/current-head Actions when the root is clean/ahead, and only emit the Blind-to-X handoff blocker when dirty paths exist. Added `workspace/tests/test_auto_research_debug_loop_inventory.py::test_build_inventory_reports_publish_boundary_when_worktree_is_clean`. Verification passed focused pytest (`4 passed`) with repo-local basetemp, related auto-research pytest (`72 passed`), Ruff check/format check, py_compile, path-limited diff-check, graph detect risk `0.00`, code-review gate pass, and A/B `adopt_candidate` (`score_delta=0.9047619047619049`). > Latest claude-goal DB path directory traceback fix: 2026-06-09 T-1732b. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after a concurrent relay already used `T-1732` for the slash simple-command extra-arg guard, so this loop uses the suffix fallback. Direct isolated CLI reproduction with `CLAUDE_GOAL_DB` pointing at an existing directory showed `status`, `json`, `doctor`, `set objective`, `invoke status`, and `stop-hook` all returned exit `1` and printed `sqlite3.OperationalError` tracebacks. Root cause: `sqlite_connect()` let expected local DB setup/connect/init failures (`OSError`/`sqlite3.Error`) escape to generic exception handlers; parsed commands printed tracebacks, and legacy `set` did not distinguish storage setup failures from command logic errors. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by wrapping storage failures in `GoalStorageError`, returning exit `2` for legacy storage failures, and handling parsed-command `ValueError` without traceback; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_db_path_directory_errors_without_traceback`. Verification passed direct after-fix CLI QA in workspace `.tmp/claude-goal-t1732b-db-path-directory-qa.json`, focused pytest (`3 passed, 109 deselected`), full isolated pytest (`162 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. This loop's temp dirs were removed. No stage, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal slash simple-command extra-arg guard: 2026-06-08 T-1732. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` CLI UX loop after T-1731. Direct isolated CLI reproduction showed direct simple state commands such as `status extra`, `pause extra`, `resume extra`, and clear aliases returned argparse exit `2`, while slash `invoke status extra` silently ran status and slash `invoke pause/resume/clear-alias extra` performed the state mutation, so a trailing typo could pause or clear an active goal. External reference checked: Python argparse invalid-argument behavior. Root cause: `_render_invoke_db_command()` handled simple invoke commands without checking `InvokeCommand.rest`; help routing already ran earlier, so trailing non-help args reached DB state changes. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding `INVOKE_SIMPLE_COMMANDS_WITHOUT_ARGS` plus a pre-DB `_reject_invoke_simple_command_args()` guard; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_simple_commands_reject_unexpected_args_without_mutating`. Verification passed direct after-fix CLI QA in workspace `.tmp/claude-goal-t1732-simple-invoke-extra-args-qa.json`, focused pytest (`3 passed, 108 deselected`), full isolated pytest (`162 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. > Latest claude-goal direct doctor negative stale-days fix: 2026-06-08 T-1731. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1730/T-1730b. Direct isolated CLI reproduction showed `doctor --stale-days -1` and `doctor --stale-days -1 --json` returned exit `0` and rendered stale reporting as though the threshold were `0` days, while slash `invoke doctor --stale-days -1` correctly returned exit `2`. Root cause: slash `parse_doctor_args()` validated negative thresholds through `_parse_stale_command_args()`, but direct `_doctor_args_from_main_namespace()` copied argparse's integer without the same non-negative check; the first fix attempt also proved namespace conversion had to sit inside `handle_main_doctor()`'s `ValueError` boundary. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by validating direct doctor stale_days and moving namespace conversion inside the clean error handler; added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_doctor_rejects_negative_stale_days_without_traceback`. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1726/T-1726b. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, refreshed the 0-stage bug inventory from current state and excluded completed T-1703 through T-1723. Highest-priority new candidate was runtime Stop-hook non-positive cap handling because it directly affects automatic continuation. Isolated active-goal reproduction showed `CLAUDE_GOAL_MAX_STOP_CONTINUES=0` and `-1` made `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py stop-hook` return a limit payload instead of the active-goal block; `-1` produced the impossible message `auto-continuation stopped after -1 Stop-hook continuations`. > Latest claude-goal direct set help non-mutating UX fix: 2026-06-08 T-1716. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, a direct CLI help mutation scan reproduced that `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py set --help` returned `0` but printed `Action: set` and stored an active goal whose objective was `--help`; `set -h` had the same state-mutation risk. Root cause: `_run_legacy_main_command()` intercepts legacy `set` before argparse, and because `set` uses raw/remainder-style objective text, `--help` was treated as objective content instead of being passed to direct `set` argparse help. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by letting legacy `set -h/--help` fall through to the existing argparse parser, and added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_set_help_is_non_mutating`. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, direct CLI use reproduced that direct subcommand help worked (`complete --help`), but slash subcommand help failed for `invoke complete --help`, `invoke contract --help`, `invoke doctor --help`, and `invoke pause-stale --help` with `unknown <command> option: --help`. > Latest claude-goal install-health runtime provenance verifier fix: 2026-06-08 T-1711. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, reproduced with a real CLI flow, temp settings, and temp goal DB that `doctor --install --verify-json <json>` accepted a strict healthy install-health JSON artifact whose `runtime.python_version` was changed to `0.0.0-tampered` while the rest of the artifact and canonical `contract_hash` stayed valid. Root cause: `_verify_install_health_runtime()` checked runtime metadata only for schema keys and nonempty strings, not for equality with the current verifier process metadata from `install_health_runtime_metadata()`. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by requiring exact runtime metadata equality and added direct CLI plus slash `invoke` regression coverage in `tests/test_claude_goal.py`. Verification passed the runtime tamper repro (`install-health runtime metadata mismatch`), focused runtime pytest (`1 passed, 93 deselected`), focused install-health verifier pytest (`14 passed, 80 deselected`), full claude-goal pytest (`135 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, diff-check with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`. Removed temp dirs; no stage, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal install-health environment path verifier fix: 2026-06-08 T-1710. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, reproduced with a real CLI flow, temp settings, and temp goal DB that `doctor --install --verify-json <json>` accepted a strict healthy install-health JSON artifact whose `settings_path` was changed to an attacker path while the rest of the artifact and canonical `contract_hash` stayed valid. > Latest claude-goal install-health Markdown artifact verifier fix: 2026-06-08 T-1701. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, reproduced with a real CLI flow, temp settings, and temp goal DB that `doctor --install --verify-json <json> --verify-markdown <md>` accepted a strict healthy install-health JSON artifact plus a garbage Markdown artifact that kept only the static `contract_hash` string and dropped the generated status/check/action report body. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, reproduced with a real CLI flow, temp settings, and temp goal DB that `doctor --install --verify-json <json> --verify-markdown <md>` accepted a strict install-health JSON artifact whose `check_counts` moved one count from `ok` to `warn` while preserving total length, original `contract_hash`, Markdown hash, and unchanged `checks` list. Actual `checks[*].status` remained `ok=18,warn=0,fail=0,info=1`, but the artifact claimed `ok=17,warn=1,fail=0,info=1`. Root cause: `_verify_install_health_check_counts()` validated nonnegative values and total length only, not the per-status distribution derived from `checks[*].status`. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` to recompute expected counts from the checks array and reject mismatched status counts; added direct CLI plus slash `invoke` regression coverage in `tests/test_claude_goal.py`. > Latest claude-goal operating-contract schema command-surface verifier fix: 2026-06-08 T-1677. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, after concurrent T-1673 through T-1676 records consumed the earlier id range, reproduced with a real CLI flow and temp goal DB that `contract --verify-json <artifact> --verify-schema <schema>` accepted a schema artifact whose `$defs.commandSurface.properties` added an unrequired `evil` command while keeping the valid operating-contract JSON artifact. Root cause: the schema verifier checked required command keys and known const values, but did not compare object-schema `properties` keys or `additionalProperties` strictness against the runtime command/current-goal surfaces. Official JSON Schema object docs confirm that `properties` defines named property schemas, unmatched properties are allowed by default, and `additionalProperties: false` closes the object surface. A/B decision: reject required-only schema verification; adopt exact required/properties/additionalProperties checks for reviewer schema artifacts. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` with `_verify_schema_object_key_surface()` and applied it to the operating-contract schema top level plus audit-step, operating-rule, command, and goal-record object schemas; added direct CLI plus slash `invoke` regression coverage in `tests/test_claude_goal.py`. > Latest claude-goal operating-contract current_goal null verifier fix: 2026-06-08 T-1674. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, reproduced that an operating-contract artifact generated with an active goal could be tampered by setting `current_goal` to `null`, then `contract --verify-json ... --verify-schema goal/docs/operating-contract-schema.json` would accept it from a different verifier session while the original goal row still existed in the SQLite DB. Root cause: the `current_goal: null` verifier path only checked the current CLI session instead of failing closed when the DB contained any goal row, allowing the artifact's session evidence to be erased. Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` with `any_goal_exists()` and made `current_goal: null` valid only when the DB has no goal rows; added regression coverage in `tests/test_claude_goal.py` for the bypass and the valid no-goal artifact case. > Latest claude-goal line-ending and pytest-isolation debug loops: 2026-06-08 T-1651. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, loop 1 reproduced a deterministic format gate failure where full pytest passed but `python -m ruff format --check goal tests` failed on `goal\scripts\claude_goal.py`; isolation showed mixed line endings (`crlf=3589`, `lf_only=103`) and `git ls-files --eol` reported `w/mixed`. Root cause was missing repo-local line-ending/Ruff policy under Windows global `core.autocrlf=true`, letting LF-only patch hunks enter CRLF files. Fixed with `claude-goal/.gitattributes`, Ruff `format.line-ending = "lf"` in `pyproject.toml`, repo-hygiene LF coverage, and Ruff normalization. Loop 2 reproduced `python -m pytest -q tests\test_repo_hygiene.py` failing because nested `/goal` pytest inherited parent workspace coverage addopts; root cause was missing local pytest config. > Latest claude-goal completion-audit verifier pipe-failure masking fix: 2026-06-08 T-1642. Continued the separate `claude-goal/` improvement loop after the CI reviewer completion-audit workflow was added. Rechecked current GitHub Actions shell behavior and Bash pipeline semantics: the prior `complete --audit-json ... | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"` pattern could let `tee` mask a verifier failure when the shell lacks `pipefail`. A/B decision: explicit `shell: bash` plus `pipefail` is smaller but keeps safety tied to shell configuration; writing verifier output to `release-evidence/goal-completion-audit.verify.txt`, appending that file to `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`, and uploading it as reviewer evidence removes the fragile pipeline. Adopted the no-pipeline artifact path. Updated `claude-goal/.github/workflows/goal-operating-contract.yml`, README, `goal/SKILL.md`, and docs tests to lock the `.verify.txt` output and reject the old `| tee` workflow. Verification passed focused docs/workflow pytest (`11 passed`), direct temp CI-flow smoke, Ruff format/check, py_compile, full claude-goal pytest (`104 passed`), diff-check with CRLF warnings only, and temp hygiene cleanup. No staging, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest auto-research selector dirty-plan freshness fix: 2026-06-08 T-1640. Reproduced a selector self-loop after refreshing `.tmp/scoped-dirty-worktree-handoff-plan-current.json` to dirty count `83`: live selector still returned `status=candidate`, selected `dirty_worktree_handoff`, and exposed `adoptable_candidate_count=1` even though the dirty handoff plan signature/path set matched the live GitHub inventory. Root cause: `_dirty_handoff_plan_matches_inventory()` treated the plan's `freshness.current` field as current-plan validity, but that field describes whether the previous plan matched the newly generated plan; a newly generated current plan can legitimately report previous-plan `stale`. Fixed `next_experiment_selector.py` to trust exact dirty-count plus dirty-path signature matching, require paths to be available, and keep freshness as evidence only. Added regression coverage for matching dirty signature with `freshness=stale`. Verification passed selector pytest (`20 passed`), related selector/debug-inventory/launch pytest (`71 passed`), Ruff format/check, py_compile, path-limited diff-check with CRLF warnings only, live selector blocked again on `dirty_worktree_handoff_current` with `adoptable_candidate_count=0`, and graph detect risk `0.00`; full dirty-tree code-review gate remains advisory WARN (`0.55`) from broad pre-existing WIP/untracked files. No staging, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, or T-251 retry.

> Latest claude-goal release-evidence repo hygiene: 2026-06-08 T-1628. In separate subrepo `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, locked down generated reviewer artifact hygiene. > Latest Word Chain UX loop: 2026-06-08 T-1602. Scoped to untouched `projects/word-chain` while preserving the dirty-handoff/T-1601 boundary. Mobile CDP browser QA at `390x844` clicked initial load, invalid one-character input, then common valid input `?čłļéĄ`. Baseline showed the app had no overflow, no small touch targets, and no failed requests, but `?čłļéĄ` ended the first turn immediately with `GAME OVER` because the AI dictionary lacked a `µĆ©? bucket. Added `µĆ©? starting words and a regression test so `pickAiWord("µĆ©?, ["?čłļéĄ"])` returns `µĆ©ņć▒ņśä`. Candidate QA now continues to an AI `µĆ©?..` word with `hasGameOver=false`. Verification passed Word Chain tests (`25 passed`), lint, build via known ASCII fallback after direct Vite Korean-path failure, path-limited diff-check with CRLF warnings only, and A/B `adopt_candidate` (`score_delta=0.5`) from `.tmp/ab-manifest-t1602-word-chain.json`. > Latest code-review gate CLI repair: 2026-06-08 T-1600. Reproduced `python execution\code_review_gate.py --base HEAD --json` failing with exit `3` and `code_review_graph is not importable from this Python interpreter`, while `py -3.13 execution\code_review_gate.py --base HEAD --json` succeeded. Root cause: the direct CLI only imported graph tools from the active interpreter, but this Windows workspace keeps `code_review_graph` available on Python 3.13 while default `python` may not have it. `execution/code_review_gate.py` now retries that specific Windows import failure once through `py -3.13`, guarded by `CODE_REVIEW_GATE_PY313_FALLBACK`, and relays stdout/stderr.
